title: DigitalClone Inbound Event
description: The single normalized payload every INPUT spoke (Slack, Discord, cron, broker poller, ...) MUST emit before handing off to the AI Hub. Spokes translate provider-specific payloads INTO this shape. The Hub never sees raw provider data except via the opaque `raw` field.

schema_version.description: Contract version. Bump on breaking changes; Hub may branch on it.

event_id.description: Unique id for THIS event, generated by the spoke.

correlation_id.description: Threads this event to its AI output and downstream actions. Reuse across a request lifecycle.

source.description: The spoke that produced the event.

type.description: Coarse intent class. 'scheduled'/'data_snapshot' come from non-human spokes (cron, broker).

occurred_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of when the event happened at the source.

actor.description: Who triggered it (omit for machine-sourced events).

channel.description: Where to reply. Maps back to a provider channel/thread.

text.description: Human-readable content / user query. Empty for pure data snapshots.

data.description: Structured payload for non-message events (e.g. broker P&L snapshot).

attachments.description: Files, images, or links normalized to {type,url,name,mime}.

raw.description: OPAQUE original provider payload. Kept for audit/debug; the Hub MUST NOT depend on it.
